are not antagonistics to the railroads,
and from the number Mr.. Farrell

Because Of the decision Of the SUI
preme court that the Northern Secur-
tiles company shall. not control both
railroads and that the officers for the
Northern Pacific road shall be chosen
by a vote of the stockholders some
doubt has been raised that President
Hill will be able to control the North
ern Pacific. Persons familiar with
the conditions. however, allege that
Mr.. Hills following can cast the vote
that will elect the president Of the
Northern Pacific. II this is true, there
Is small doubt but that Mr.. Farrell
can be the president OF the Northern
Pacific If he desires, and that he can
be the man who will select the rail-
road candidates for governor and sem
ator in this state. From the part
that Mr. Farrell has already taken In
state politics there is little doubt but
that he would pick the right men
from the standpoint of his superiors.
